title: Calytrix tenuiramea
taxonomy: Calytrix tenuiramea is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Calytrix, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. Calytrix tenuiramea specifically is part of the Myrtales order. Myrtaceae is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. The classification of Calytrix tenuiramea reflects its evolutionary relationships to other plants within the Myrtaceae family.
uses: Calytrix tenuiramea has been traditionally used in Australian Indigenous medicine, but its specific medicinal uses are not well-documented in mainstream scientific literature. The plant has been used in various ways, including as a treatment for colds and fever. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal properties and benefits. Some studies suggest that Calytrix tenuiramea may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is required to confirm these findings and explore potential applications.

preparations: Calytrix tenuiramea is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional herbal medicine.  In Australia, where it is native, its leaves and stems are used to make infusions and decoctions for medicinal purposes.  The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional uses.  However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits. intro: Calytrix tenuiramea, also known as the delicate fringe-lily or white fringe-lily, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as antioxidant and wound-healing properties. The bioactive constituents of Calytrix tenuiramea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Western Australian medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
